title: valeriana wallrothii subsp. nitida
intro: Valeriana wallrothii subsp. nitida is a subspecies of the Valeriana genus of flowering plants, commonly known as the valerian or valerian root, which is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its sedative and anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and isovaleric acid. The health benefits of Valeriana wallrothii subsp. nitida include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety levels, and relief from muscle and joint pain. Herbal preparations of Valeriana wallrothii subsp. nitida can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, capsules, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation and reduce sleep disorders.
